change_comments.message:
also we might need to update the postman collection we already have.
and since this is backward compatible change I assume DT is OK with this change.

changes looks ok to me.
Also you do accept the risk of Non backward compatible nature of this change. right?
Please restore the openapi files as well because they contain generated code.
apart from this comment , i think m good.
exception handling needs to done properly. other changes looks fine to me.
move back to active once you have incorporated the comments.

inline_comments.message: can also use jsonObjectMapper for this ???
this can be part of a method.
Used ObjectMapper so that in can able to call this method. let me know if any changes needed

No. we cant do this.
We already have JsonObjectMapper dependency.
If something is not present in that then maybe implement it over there and use that.

Also u dont need a static instance of the same. You can have a non static method to use the jsonObjectMapper as is.
now it wont be part of the payload. so do we have to change the method name as well. @Arpit looking for your opinion as well as you originally introduced it.
also is it possible to have XML data as well.?? is it in scope?
is this needed or we can direcly return jsonContent directly ??
NB: in groovy make sure we use single quote instead of double quote.
As of now json data, XML is not supported
jsonContent doesn't check for validation if invalid json then jsonNode perform validation checks.

After internal discussions, we've decided not to change the method name, the jsonFile is still a form of payload but in different format i.e, file, we felt the existing method name still reflects its purpose and doesn't need modification
extra copyright not needed. can update the year in the existing copyright.
NB: but here the test has a multipart file. right ??

NB: also can reduce the long commit message.
can include in the summary section.

NB: do we also need to check the extension of the file ( *.json ) ?? Or we just rely on the user that it will provide json content in any file?

for now any file content is accepted I guess.

i think we need a custom exception here with relevant name? what do u guys think?

customexception extends RuntimeException .... like this. See other exceptions in the repo.
this might change if you incorporate my other comment.
As of now it will be fine again we will bring up for support of XML
NB: for invalid json file maybe ??
NB: add reference for both the files from components.yml
NB: jsonFile for clear differentiation also change in required field below
NB: schemaSetFile or yangResourceFile, to differentiate from jsonFile
I would prefer it to be part of MultipartFileUtil.java as the code there handles file types like yang and zip files.
NB: multipartJsonFile same in other places
We can keep it as it is for now, we will have to rename it once XML support in delta is being worked on

instead of instantiating it like this , you can as well accept the reference of jsonObjectMapper from the controller layer where the bean is been injected.

public static String extractJsonContent(final MultipartFile jsonFile, final JsonObjectMapper jsonObjectMapper) ...

This block tests for no file provided and empty file.
An IOException should be thrown here as this is an operational error.
ANd the unit tests need to be adjusted accordingly.
@Priyank can you look into this and provide your feedback

IOException should be caught when no/empty file is provided. But here you are validating the JSON string using the jsonObjectMapper. So this should be a DataValidationException.

@priyank you can provide inputs here.
This is not required. The tests should focus on testing delta rest controller. No need to test behavior of delta service.
yes, here we are validating where the payload is present or empty(depends on the user input)which is similar to validation error not a failure in reading file
jsonFile.getBytes()can throw IO exception, since it is involved in I/O operations i.e, reading uploaded file content so preferred IO exception here.
we cant be throwing any checked exception to the end user in my opinion , thats why I suggested to use a custom exception.

no. we dont send back the error response like this.
maybe use the CpsRestExceptionHandler to handle the error scenario. and also the checked exception needs to be translated to unchecked exception.
can also verify the http status code?

This test is a bit confusing. An empty json file would mean a file with no content is provided as payload. So the exception thrown should be DataValidationException with message: "Failed to read JSON file"

You should provide two test cases as follows:
- Get delta between anchor and JSON payload with no or invalid content: tests for DataValidation Exception

- Get delta between anchor and missing JSON payload: tests for file not found exception

Initially i added data validation for Jsonfile with no content because we were checking for data is present or not. However since jsonFile is a required parameter, i don't think we need to validate its presence. It should always be available otherwise we receive the default MissingServletRequestPartException.

NB: woud like a more specific name
sugggestion: targetDataAsJsonFile
multipartFile, follow the classname is better , you still have to confirm the content and confirm tis a valid json file...

is this possible using REST interface or would it be an invalid request and never get here?

I think null is NOT posisble but empty file is...
what would happen if the file does NOT contain valid json?! Still an IO exception?!

so the scenario (and title of tshi test) is something like

'Attempt to get delta with empty file'

what happens : IO exception does not need to be i the title, it is in the then part. 

PS I would like to see an additional test:
'Attempt to get delta with file with invalid json'
(the file content could just be '{')
This file is build during mvn install. I dont think it should have changed but it is not your issue. I get SOurabh to look into this...
we may add XML data as well and convertToJsonNode handles the parsing to it verifies the json data
yes, jsonfile can be empty only

Invalid Json is handled in the convertToJsonNode method, where a JsonProcessingException is caught and rethrown as a DataValidationException. So instead of an IOException, we receive DatavalidationException, which indicated parsing issue

there is some issue with this file.
or you are not working on the latest code.
rebase the code.
do a mvn clean install locally.
and commit the changes the related to openapi files that are generated.
NB Tip: For test that expect to end in an error scenario i always use the term 'Attempt' e.g 'Attempt to get delta between anchor and empty file'
